summary: Frontend-only PWA Install App Button testing for Servall CRM. Verified that InstallAppButton silently renders nothing in default chromium (preview env, no beforeinstallprompt), and renders correctly under iOS Safari user-agent simulation with working dialog flow. Regression smoke for /funnel, /users, /leads also passing. No backend tests run (per request).
critical_code_review_comments: InstallAppButton.jsx: `isStandalone` is computed at render time (not in state) — this is fine because it's stable across renders, but adding it to useEffect deps array is harmless. No issue.

Two install-app-btn elements with the same data-testid are rendered (mobile topbar + desktop topbar). Only one is visible at any given viewport due to `hidden md:flex` parent classes, so it works in practice. However, for stricter testability, consider giving them distinct testids (e.g., install-app-btn-mobile / install-app-btn-desktop) or de-duplicating via a single mount point with responsive styling. Minor maintainability nit only.
iOS detection via UA regex is the standard approach but will miss edge cases (e.g., iPadOS 13+ reports as Mac). Acceptable for this MVP feature.

Service worker registration is gated by NODE_ENV==='production' — so install button via beforeinstallprompt path is impossible to verify in preview/dev mode. iOS UA-help path is the only testable path here, and it works correctly.

test_results.default_chromium_desktop_login: PASS — logged in to /dashboard, no console errors related to InstallAppButton
test_results.install_btn_hidden_in_default_chromium: PASS — [data-testid='install-app-btn'] returns null (canShow=false because no beforeinstallprompt fired and !isIOS)
test_results.ios_safari_ua_simulation_login: PASS — fresh Playwright context with iPhone 17 UA + 390x844 viewport logged in successfully
test_results.install_btn_visible_on_ios_ua: PASS — install-app-btn rendered, visible=True, bbox present in mobile topbar
test_results.ios_help_dialog_opens_on_click: PASS — clicking the visible install-app-btn opens [data-testid='ios-install-help'] (Radix Dialog portal), visible=True
test_results.ios_help_dialog_has_3_steps: PASS — 3 numbered step badges (span.bg-brand) found inside dialog
test_results.regression_funnel_page: PASS — /funnel renders Sales Funnel kanban with INQUIRY (6 leads), FOLLOW-UP (2 leads), HOLD, BOOKING, DELIVERY, ALLOTTED columns visible (8 stages, horizontally scrollable)
test_results.regression_users_delete_btn: PASS — /users page renders with 4 delete buttons for non-self super_admin rows
test_results.regression_leads_list: PASS — /leads page shows 8 leads with correct stage badges (INQUIRY/FOLLOW-UP) and priority chips
test_results.console_errors_on_default_chromium: 0 InstallAppButton-related errors. Background 401 errors observed on iOS context (likely a pre-auth resource fetch); unrelated to install button feature.

context_for_next_testing_agent: PWA InstallAppButton iter-23 verified end-to-end. Default chromium (preview/dev mode) correctly hides the button (canShow=false). iOS Safari UA simulation via new browser context with user_agent='Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 17_0...) Safari/604.1' + viewport 390x844 + is_mobile=True correctly shows the button and opens the help dialog with 3 steps + close button. NOTE: Two install-app-btn elements exist in the DOM (mobile + desktop topbar), only one visible per viewport — when writing future Playwright tests, always loop and pick the visible one (or use is_mobile context). beforeinstallprompt path is NOT testable in preview env because SW registration is gated by NODE_ENV==='production'. Backend untouched in this iter.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs surfaced. One initial flaky test where dialog appeared not to open after click (force=True) — re-running with normal click after scroll_into_view reliably opened the dialog with all expected content. The flake is most likely a timing/portal-mount race in Playwright's force=True dispatch path, not a product bug.
